benchmarkModels.purpose: Measure local syntax, smoke, bridge, queue, and browser QA workload across four operation modes.
benchmarkModels.whenToUse: Before changing harness defaults or after QA automation changes.

benchmarkModels.purpose: Compare documentation/template work where planning, writing, review, and evidence collection can split naturally.
benchmarkModels.whenToUse: When tuning harness behavior for docs-heavy operations.

benchmarkModels.purpose: Measure security-preflight, implementation, review, and validation separation for sensitive local storage/auth work.
benchmarkModels.whenToUse: Before accepting sensitive-data MVP changes or modifying auth/encryption behavior.

benchmarkModels.purpose: Compare ledger, Discord/OpenClaw, and queue dispatch dry-runs without live side effects.
benchmarkModels.whenToUse: Before enabling live dispatch, GitHub issue mutation, or Discord thread updates.

benchmarkModels.purpose: Compare diagnosis speed and fix quality for failing tests, port conflicts, race conditions, and integration defects.
benchmarkModels.whenToUse: When failures recur or harness routing may reduce debugging time.

benchmarkModels.purpose: Compare release packet, QA checklist, security gate, evidence summary, and handoff review distribution.
benchmarkModels.whenToUse: Before external handoff, deployment readiness decisions, or pilot release.

benchmarkModels.purpose: Measure sustained queue processing, rollback behavior, rate-limit behavior, and failure recovery over longer unattended runs.
benchmarkModels.whenToUse: Only when the user explicitly starts a longer benchmark window.

notes: Thresholds are per task family and mode; do not compare unrelated task families directly.
Provisional thresholds are useful for warning, not hard gating.
Wall-clock data is local environment dependent and should be compared against the same machine/session class.
